name: Black Hills
description_short: CUScore is an independent directory and comparison tool for federally insured credit unions, providing NCUA financial data, health scores, and branch location information for 4,374 credit unions across America.
description_long:
CUScore operates as a comprehensive directory and research platform for credit union discovery and comparison in the United States. The platform aggregates and publishes quarterly financial data from the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), enabling consumers and researchers to explore the credit union industry landscape across all 50 states, Washington D.C., and U.S. territories. The site explicitly states it is an independent directory not affiliated with the NCUA or any individual credit union.

The platform offers several core features for credit union research and comparison. Users can browse and compare 4,374 federally insured credit unions using metrics including total assets, membership size, net worth ratios, and financial health scores. The directory provides state-by-state browsing capabilities and national rankings that highlight the largest credit unions by total assets. The site includes detailed comparative data showing the industry encompasses 146 million members nationwide with combined assets of $2.456 trillion as of Q4 2025, and maintains an average net worth ratio of 11.2% across the industry.

CUScore distinguishes itself through its educational approach and data transparency. Beyond the directory function, the platform provides educational guides comparing credit unions to banks, explaining how to join a credit union, and defining what credit unions are and how they operate. The comparison charts highlight structural differences in ownership, purpose, deposit insurance, interest rates, fees, and governance between credit unions and traditional banks. All financial data is sourced directly from NCUA Quarterly Call Reports and clearly dated, providing verifiable, official regulatory information rather than proprietary analysis.

The platform serves as a reference tool rather than a service provider. CUScore does not offer accounts, loans, or financial products—it functions purely as an information aggregator and comparison directory. Users seeking to join a credit union must contact individual institutions directly. The value proposition relies entirely on data accuracy, comprehensiveness of the directory, and educational content quality. This positions CUScore as a research utility for consumers comparing credit union options rather than a financial services provider.
services: Directory search of 4,374 federally insured credit unions with NCUA financial data
Financial health scoring and ratings for credit unions
State-by-state credit union browsing and filtering
National rankings of credit unions by total assets
Branch location information for credit unions
Quarterly financial data including total assets, membership counts, and net worth ratios
Educational guide: What Is a Credit Union
Educational guide: Credit Union vs. Bank comparison
Educational guide: How to Join a Credit Union
Industry statistics and aggregate data (146M members, $2.456T assets, 11.2% avg net worth ratio)
NCUA regulatory compliance information and insurance details
Credit union popular selection highlights and top 10 largest rankings
pros: Comprehensive directory covering 4,374 federally insured credit unions with complete NCUA regulatory data
Provides objective health scores and financial metrics for comparing credit union stability and performance
State-by-state browsing with nationwide rankings by total assets, enabling targeted searches
Free educational guides explaining credit union structures, membership requirements, and CU vs. bank comparisons
Data sourced directly from official NCUA Quarterly Call Reports, ensuring regulatory accuracy and transparency
Clearly states independence from NCUA and individual credit unions, reducing bias concerns
Updated quarterly with current financial data (Q4 2025 as of March 2026), maintaining relevance
cons: Directory only—cannot open accounts, apply for loans, or conduct transactions directly through the platform
State-level asset totals display as $0, suggesting incomplete data presentation or technical issues in the interface
Limited depth on individual credit union product offerings, rates, or membership eligibility criteria
No personalized recommendation engine—users must manually compare institutions without decision support
Mobile functionality and user experience not evident from website content; unclear how accessible platform is across devices
best_for: Consumers researching which credit union to join by comparing financial health and size across states
Individuals learning the differences between credit unions and traditional banks before deciding where to bank
Researchers and analysts needing authoritative NCUA financial data and credit union industry benchmarks
People relocating who need to find federally insured credit unions in their new state
diagnosis: CUScore is best for consumers and researchers seeking objective, regulatory-based information to compare and evaluate credit unions before joining. The key caveat is that CUScore is a reference tool only—it provides no account opening, lending, or transactional services, so users must contact credit unions directly to actually join or apply for products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
